compiled_state.title: Write RED structural tests for consumer_completeness dimension in scope reviewer
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Testing Mode
RED

## TDD Requirement
Create tests/scripts/test-scope-reviewer-consumer-completeness.sh with a single structural boundary test (Rule 5 compliant — Contract schema validation category) that FAILS RED before consumer_completeness is added to scope.md.

Rule 5 explicitly allows: required JSON/YAML schema keys that define the output contract.
The test checks that 'consumer_completeness' exists as a key in scope.md's JSON output schema — this IS the structural contract, not body text.

Test function:
- test_scope_reviewer_has_consumer_completeness_dimension:
  Check that '"consumer_completeness":' appears inside scope.md's JSON dimensions block.
  Use awk to scope to the JSON block: awk '/"dimensions": \{/,/^\}/' scope.md | grep -q '"consumer_completeness":'
  Currently: key does not exist → RED
  After task 2: key exists → GREEN

Placement: tests/scripts/ — matching existing scope.md tests (test-isolation-rule-no-unscoped-export.sh, test-sprint-review-scope-check.sh)

## Implementation Steps
1. Create tests/scripts/test-scope-reviewer-consumer-completeness.sh following pattern of test-epic-scrutiny-pipeline.sh
   Variables: REPO_ROOT, SCOPE_MD="$REPO_ROOT/plugins/dso/skills/shared/docs/reviewers/scope.md"
2. Implement test_scope_reviewer_has_consumer_completeness_dimension():
   Given: scope.md exists
   When: check the JSON dimensions block for the 'consumer_completeness' key
   Then: pass if found, fail if missing
   Implementation:
     if [ ! -f "$SCOPE_MD" ]; then fail "scope.md missing"; return; fi
     if awk '/"dimensions": \{/,/^\}/' "$SCOPE_MD" | grep -q '"consumer_completeness":'; then
       pass "scope.md JSON schema includes consumer_completeness dimension key"
     else
       fail "scope.md JSON schema missing consumer_completeness dimension key"
     fi
3. chmod +x tests/scripts/test-scope-reviewer-consumer-completeness.sh
4. Add NEW SEPARATE LINE to .test-index for the RED phase (per convention — RED markers are on their own line):
   Add: plugins/dso/skills/shared/docs/reviewers/scope.md:tests/scripts/test-scope-reviewer-consumer-completeness.sh [test_scope_reviewer_has_consumer_completeness_dimension]
   This temporarily creates a 3rd entry for scope.md; task d954-d23a removes it and merges to line 316
5. Verify bash tests/scripts/test-scope-reviewer-consumer-completeness.sh exits non-zero (RED)

## File Impact
- tests/scripts/test-scope-reviewer-consumer-completeness.sh: create (new test file)
- .test-index: add new separate line (3rd entry for scope.md during RED phase)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tests/scripts/test-scope-reviewer-consumer-completeness.sh exists and is executable
  Verify: [ -x tests/scripts/test-scope-reviewer-consumer-completeness.sh ]
- [ ] test_scope_reviewer_has_consumer_completeness_dimension function exists
  Verify: grep -q 'test_scope_reviewer_has_consumer_completeness_dimension' tests/scripts/test-scope-reviewer-consumer-completeness.sh
- [ ] .test-index has RED marker line for test-scope-reviewer-consumer-completeness.sh
  Verify: grep -q 'test-scope-reviewer-consumer-completeness.sh \[test_scope_reviewer_has_consumer_completeness_dimension\]' .test-index
- [ ] RED confirmation: test file exits non-zero before scope.md changes
  Verify: bash tests/scripts/test-scope-reviewer-consumer-completeness.sh; [ $? -ne 0 ]

comments.body: PLAN REVISION: Redesigning tests to use structural boundary assertions (Rule 5 compliant) instead of grepping for 'consumer_completeness' string in body text. Also fixing .test-index: must append to line 316 not create new entry.
comments.author: Test
